login.inviteExpired: This invite link is invalid or has expired.
login.ssoOnlyDesc: Password login is disabled on this server.

totp.title: Two-factor auth
totp.instructions: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app.

emailOtp.title: Check your email
emailOtp.desc: A verification code has been sent to {{email}}

mfaEnroll.title: Set up two-factor authentication
mfaEnroll.desc: Your organization requires two-factor authentication. Please set up an authenticator app to continue.
mfaEnroll.scanInstructions: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Authy).
mfaEnroll.manualKey: Can’t scan? Enter this key manually:
mfaEnroll.verifyInstructions: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to confirm setup.

forgotPassword.title: Reset your password
forgotPassword.desc: Enter your recovery email address and we’ll send you a reset link.
forgotPassword.sentDesc: If an account with that recovery email exists, you’ll receive a reset link shortly.

resetPassword.title: Set new password
resetPassword.tooShort: Password must be at least 8 characters
resetPassword.doneDesc: Your password has been updated. You can now sign in.

compose.subject: Subject
compose.draftHasAttachments: Attachments are not saved with drafts. They will need to be re-attached when you reopen this draft.

forgottenAttachment.title: Forgot an attachment?
forgottenAttachment.body: Your message mentions an attachment but nothing is attached.

archived.title: Message archived
archived.noFolderBody: No archive folder configured for this account. Set one in Settings → Accounts → Folder Mappings.

messageList.moveToFolderDisabled: Select messages from one account to move

bulkArchived.noFolderBody: One or more accounts have no archive folder configured. Set one in Settings → Accounts → Folder Mappings.

noArchiveFolder.title: No archive folder
noArchiveFolder.body: No archive folder configured. Set one in Settings → Accounts → Folder Mappings.

messageList.noAccountsDesc: Add an email account to get started

sidebar.confirmDelete: Delete folder "{{name}}"? All messages in it will be permanently deleted.
sidebar.confirmEmpty: Empty "{{name}}"? All messages in this folder will be permanently deleted.

imageTooLarge.title: Image too large
imageTooLarge.body: Must be under 300 KB. Use an external URL for larger images.

profile.title: Edit Profile
profile.displayNameHint: Shown instead of your username. Leave blank to use username.

rules.title: Inbox rules
rules.empty: No rules yet. Rules automatically filter incoming mail.
rules.bodyConditionNote: Body rules only run if the message body is already downloaded. If the body has not been fetched yet when a message arrives, the rule is skipped and the message stays in your inbox — body rules do not apply retroactively.
rules.actionMovePlaceholder: Exact IMAP folder path (e.g. Work or INBOX/Work)
rules.stopProcessing: Stop processing further rules if this rule matches
rules.stopProcessingHint: If this rule matches, later rules won't run for the same message.
rules.errorRequired: Rule name and at least one condition and action are required.
rules.errorMoveFolder: Please enter a destination folder for the Move action.

blockList.description: Emails from blocked senders are automatically moved to trash.
blockList.errorAdd: Failed to add to block list

accounts.title: Email accounts
accounts.skipTlsVerifyDesc: Accept self-signed certificates. Use only for servers on your local network.
accounts.errorRequired: Email, username, and IMAP host are required
accounts.errorPasswordRequired: Password is required for new accounts
accounts.microsoftImapUnsupported: IMAP not available for Outlook.com / Hotmail
accounts.microsoftImapNote: Microsoft has permanently disabled password-based IMAP for personal accounts. There is no workaround — this is a Microsoft policy. Consider using Gmail, iCloud, or Fastmail, which support standard IMAP with app passwords.

folderMappings.title: Folder mappings
folderMappings.description: Map each mail role to a specific folder. Select Auto-detect to let MailFlow use the folder your server has tagged for that role.

aliases.title: Aliases
aliases.description: Aliases let you send from a different name or address using this account's SMTP credentials. Select an alias in the From field when composing. MailFlow will automatically reply from an alias if a message was addressed to it.
aliases.name: Display name
aliases.replyToPh: Leave blank to use the alias address
aliases.signatureNote: Optional — leave blank to inherit the account signature.
aliases.deleteConfirmBody: This alias will be permanently removed. This cannot be undone.

appearance.title: Appearance
appearance.description: Choose a color theme for the interface
appearance.typographyDescription: Choose a font pairing for the interface
appearance.layoutDescription: Choose how the sidebar, message list, and reading pane are arranged
appearance.languageDescription: Choose the display language for the interface
appearance.fontSizeDescription: Scale the interface text. 100% is the default.

messageList.title: Message List
messageList.syncFrequencyDesc: How often MailFlow checks for new emails
messageList.composeRichTextDesc: Sends HTML — supports formatting and styled signatures
messageList.defaultReplyDesc: The Reply button sends only to the sender
messageList.defaultReplyAllDesc: The Reply button sends to all recipients
messageList.hoverQuickActionsOffDesc: Keep Quick Actions hidden until you use the context menu or shortcuts.
messageList.hoverQuickActionsOnDesc: Show Quick Action icons in message rows when the pointer is over them.

notifications.title: Notifications
notifications.description: Choose a sound to play when new mail arrives. Click a card to select and preview.
notifications.badgeDescription: Control where the unread count is displayed.
notifications.appBadgeDesc: Unread count on the taskbar or dock icon when installed as a PWA.
notifications.faviconBadgeDesc: Unread count on the browser tab icon.

push.title: Push Notifications
push.description: Receive native notifications for new mail even when MailFlow isn't open.
push.permissionDenied: Notification permission was denied. Please enable it in your browser or system settings.
push.notSupported: Push notifications are not supported in this browser.
push.iosHint: To receive push notifications on iOS, add MailFlow to your Home Screen first: tap the Share button, then "Add to Home Screen".
push.notConfigured: Push notifications are not configured on this server. Add VAPID keys to the .env file to enable them.

security.title: Security
security.description: Manage two-factor authentication for your account.
security.totpScanInstructions: Scan this QR code with your authenticator app (e.g. Google Authenticator, Authy).
security.totpManualKey: Can't scan? Enter this key manually:
security.totpVerifyInstructions: Enter the 6-digit code from your authenticator app to confirm setup.
security.totpDisableInstructions: Enter your password to disable two-factor authentication.
security.ssoDescription: Connect your account to an SSO provider so you can sign in without a password.
security.ssoUnlinkBody: You will no longer be able to sign in with this provider. Make sure you have another way to sign in.
security.ssoNoProviders: No SSO providers are configured on this server.
security.loginProtectionDesc: Failed login attempts allowed from a single IP address before it is temporarily blocked.
security.activityDesc: Recent authentication events across all users.
security.mailPolicyDesc: Controls which connection types users are permitted to configure. Disable these for internet-facing deployments.
security.allowPrivateHostsDesc: Permits connecting to IMAP and SMTP servers at private or local addresses (e.g. 127.0.0.1, 192.168.x.x). Required for protonmail-bridge and similar local mail bridges.
security.allowInsecureTlsDesc: Allows users to disable TLS certificate verification per account. Required for servers with self-signed certificates.
security.allowNonstandardPortsDesc: Allows any IMAP or SMTP port, not just 143/993 (IMAP) and 465/587 (SMTP). Required for servers like protonmail-bridge that use non-standard ports.
security.mfaEnforcementDesc: Require all users to verify with a second factor when signing in.
security.mfaDeviceTrustDesc: How long a verified device is trusted before requiring 2FA again. Only applies when MFA is required.
security.mfaSsoLockedNotice: MFA enforcement is not applicable when password login is disabled. Users authenticate via SSO, which manages its own MFA requirements.
security.recoveryEmailDesc: Receive a sign-in code by email if you lose access to your authenticator app.

privacy.title: Privacy
privacy.description: Control whether remote images in emails are loaded automatically
privacy.blockImagesDesc: Prevents tracking pixels and remote image loading by default
privacy.allowedSendersDesc: Images are always loaded from these sender addresses
privacy.allowedDomainsDesc: Images are always loaded from senders whose email is at these domains

shortcuts.title: Keyboard Shortcuts
shortcuts.description: Click a key binding to reassign it. Press Esc to cancel recording.
shortcuts.conflict: Key {{key}} was already assigned to {{action}} — it has been reassigned.
shortcuts.footer: Press ? anywhere in the app to see the current shortcut reference.

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage user accounts and admin privileges
users.registrationOpenDesc: When enabled, anyone can create an account without an invite link.
users.registrationStatusClosed: Only users with an invite link can register
users.inviteDescription: Generate a single-use invite link valid for 7 days. An email will be sent using the system SMTP account (or your first personal SMTP account as a fallback).
users.inviteCreatedNoEmail: Invite created (email not sent — no SMTP account configured).
users.deleteConfirmBody: This user account will be permanently deleted. This cannot be undone.
users.disable2faConfirmBody: This will remove their authenticator app requirement. They can re-enable it themselves.

systemEmail.description: Configure a dedicated SMTP account for sending invite emails and other system messages. If not set, the admin's first personal SMTP account is used as a fallback.
systemEmail.passPlaceholder: Leave blank to keep current password

sso.title: Single Sign-On
sso.description: Configure external OIDC providers so users can sign in with their organization accounts.
sso.passwordLoginDesc: When disabled, users can only sign in via SSO. Requires at least one enabled SSO provider and your admin account must be linked to an SSO identity.
sso.passwordLoginEnabledDesc: Users can sign in with username and password
sso.passwordLoginDisabledDesc: Users must sign in via SSO
sso.passwordLoginDisableMsg: Users will only be able to sign in via SSO. If your SSO provider becomes unavailable, direct database access will be required to re-enable password login.
sso.templatePickerDesc: Select a pre-configured template to get started quickly, or choose Custom to configure manually.
sso.name: Provider name
sso.domains: Allowed email domains (comma-separated, leave blank for any)
sso.provisioningDisabled: Disabled — existing linked identities can still log in, but no new links
sso.provisioningExisting: Match existing — link to a MailFlow account with a matching email
sso.provisioningOpen: Open — create a new account automatically on first SSO login
sso.requireEmailVerifiedDesc: Disable for self-hosted providers (PocketID, Kanidm, etc.) that do not issue email_verified: true in their tokens.
sso.allowInsecureDesc: Bypasses host validation and TLS certificate checks. Use only for LAN-only providers (e.g. pocketid.internal). Not recommended for internet-facing deployments.
sso.deleteConfirmBody: All linked user identities for this provider will also be removed. Users with no other login method will be locked out.
sso.errorRequired: Name, slug, issuer URL and client ID are required.

microsoft.title: Microsoft 365 / Outlook.com
microsoft.description: OAuth2 for work, school, or personal Microsoft accounts (Outlook.com, Hotmail)
microsoft.step1: Go to portal.azure.com → Microsoft Entra ID → App registrations → New registration. Under Supported account types, select 'Accounts in any organizational directory and personal Microsoft accounts' to allow Outlook.com/Hotmail logins
microsoft.step2: Set redirect URI type to Web and paste the URI shown below
microsoft.step3: API permissions → Add a permission → APIs my organization uses (search 'Exchange' if not listed) → Office 365 Exchange Online → Delegated → IMAP.AccessAsUser.All and SMTP.Send
microsoft.step4: API permissions → Add a permission → Microsoft Graph → Delegated → offline_access, openid, email, profile
microsoft.step5: Click Grant admin consent for your organization (org/work accounts only — personal accounts skip this step)
microsoft.step6: Certificates & secrets → New client secret — copy the Value (not the ID)
microsoft.tenantIdNote: Use 'consumers' for personal Outlook.com/Hotmail only, 'common' for both personal and org, or your tenant UUID for a single organization
microsoft.redirectUriNote: This must exactly match the redirect URI registered in Azure. Suggested: {{uri}}
microsoft.savedNote: Configuration saved. Click "Connect account" to authorize.
microsoft.connectedNote: Microsoft 365 account connected successfully! Check the Accounts tab.
microsoft.deviceCodeNote: No redirect URI or client secret needed. In Azure, go to Authentication and set 'Allow public client flows' to Yes. Best for personal Outlook.com / Hotmail accounts.
microsoft.deviceCodeInstructions: Visit the link below on any device and enter the code to authorize MailFlow:
microsoft.deviceCodeError: An error occurred. Please try again.

todoist.title: Todoist
todoist.help: Find your token in Todoist Settings → Integrations → Developer

ssoLinked.title: SSO identity linked
ssoLinked.body: Your account is now linked to the SSO provider.

spam.failHamTitle: Could not mark as not spam
spam.failBody: The mail server rejected the move.
spam.failHamBody: The mail server rejected the move.

contacts.title: Contacts
contacts.selectHint: Select a contact to view details
